title: How The Enhabit (EHAB) Story Is Shifting Toward Kinderhook’s US$13.80 Cash Deal
content:
Track your investments for FREE with Simply Wall St, the portfolio command center trusted by over 7 million individual investors worldwide.

The consensus fair value estimate for Enhabit has shifted only slightly, from about US$13.80 to roughly US$13.83 per share, staying closely in line with the agreed US$13.80 cash offer. That tiny move matches the way recent analyst commentary has pivoted toward the deal price itself, with target changes largely reflecting the acquisition terms rather than a fresh view on long term potential. As you read on, you will see how this tighter price anchoring shapes the evolving analyst narrative and what it means for tracking the stock from here.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value Enhabit.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Several firms, including Leerink and Truist, moved their price targets up to US$13.80, aligning with the Kinderhook cash offer and signaling that the deal terms are viewed as fair relative to prior targets of US$9.50 and US$10.50. Earlier in January, TD Cowen lifted its target from US$9 to US$12 and moved to Buy, pointing to an improving home health regulatory backdrop and what it described as durable volume trends for Enhabit.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Following the acquisition announcement, TD Cowen, UBS, Jefferies, and Oppenheimer all shifted to more neutral stances, moving ratings to Hold, Neutral, or Perform and anchoring price targets at the US$13.80 offer level. These rating changes suggest analysts see limited upside beyond the agreed cash consideration, with focus now centered on deal completion rather than further rerating on execution or growth prospects as a standalone company.

We've flagged 1 risk for Enhabit. See which could impact your investment.

What's in the News

Kinderhook Industries, LLC agreed to acquire Enhabit, Inc. for about US$740 million, with stockholders set to receive US$13.80 per share in cash under a definitive agreement signed on February 22, 2026. The acquisition is backed by committed debt financing and equity commitments from funds advised by Kinderhook, indicating that funding has been fully arranged for the proposed deal. Both Boards of Directors unanimously approved the transaction, which is targeted to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to Enhabit stockholder approval, Hart Scott Rodino clearance, regulatory approvals, and other customary conditions. The merger agreement includes reciprocal termination fees, with Enhabit responsible for US$24.5 million and Kinderhook responsible for US$44.6 million if certain termination conditions occur. Enhabit also reported goodwill impairments of US$44.7 million and intangible asset impairments of US$3 million for the fourth quarter of 2025.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For Enhabit

Fair value estimate has shifted slightly from about US$13.80 to roughly US$13.83 per share, remaining closely aligned with the US$13.80 cash offer. Revenue growth input remains effectively unchanged at about 5.38%. Net profit margin assumption is essentially flat at roughly 2.32%, with only a very small numerical adjustment. Future P/E multiple is almost unchanged, moving slightly from about 29.48x to roughly 29.50x. Modeled discount rate is marginally lower, shifting from about 7.09% to roughly 7.04%.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives link Enhabit's business story to analyst forecasts and fair value, updating as new data, guidance, and news come through. They help you see how clinical, regulatory, and financial factors fit together in one place.

How aging demographics and a shift to in home care support Enhabit's home health and hospice volumes over time. What payer contract renegotiations, value based models, and technology investments could mean for margins and earnings stability.
